title: Hanle Monastery
tagline: Hanle Monastery — spiritual town of Ladakh (UT)

overview.short: Hanle Monastery is a 17th-century gompa of the Drukpa Lineage of the Kagyu school of Tibetan Buddhism located in the Hanle Valley, Leh district, Ladakh, India on an old branch of t
overview.description: Hanle Monastery is a 17th-century gompa of the Drukpa Lineage of the Kagyu school of Tibetan Buddhism located in the Hanle Valley, Leh district, Ladakh, India on an old branch of the ancient Ladakh-Tibet trade route. The valley is home to about a thousand people, with about 300 people living in Hanle village. The monastery is home to about ten monks while another 33 or so come regularly for prayers. It is only 19 kilometres (12 mi) from the disputed frontier between India and Chinese-controlled Tibet.

topPlaces.name: Hanle (village)
topPlaces.description: Hanle, also spelt Anle, is a large historic village in the Changthang district of the Indian union territory of Ladakh. It is located in the Hanle River valley on an old branch of the ancient Ladakh–Tibet trade route.

topPlaces.name: Major Atmospheric Cerenkov Experiment Telescope
topPlaces.description: Major Atmospheric Cerenkov Experiment Telescope (MACE) is an imaging atmospheric Cerenkov telescope (IACT) located near Hanle, Ladakh, India. It is the highest (in altitude) and second largest Cerenkov telescope in the world.

topPlaces.name: Anlay
topPlaces.description: Anlay is a village in the Changthang district in the Indian union territory of Ladakh. It is located in the Nyoma tehsil.

items.note: Hanle, also spelt Anle, is a large historic village in the Changthang district of the Indian union territory of Ladakh. It is located in the Hanle River valley on an old branch of the ancient Ladakh–Tibet trade route.

Major Atmospheric Cerenkov Experiment Telescope (MACE) is an imaging atmospheric Cerenkov telescope (IACT) located near Hanle, Ladakh, India. It is the highest (in altitude) and second largest Cerenkov telescope in the world.
Anlay is a village in the Changthang district in the Indian union territory of Ladakh. It is located in the Nyoma tehsil.

faq.q: What is the best time to visit Hanle Monastery?
faq.a: The best time to visit Hanle Monastery is Mar – Jun, Sep – Nov. Summers are 15–28°C and winters are 2–16°C.

faq.q: How do I reach Hanle Monastery?
faq.a: The nearest airport is Kullu-Manali Airport (Bhuntar) (201 km) and the nearest railway station is Dehradun Railway Station (290 km). Roads are generally good; check seasonal/monsoon conditions before high-altitude or remote stretches.

faq.q: How many days are enough for Hanle Monastery?
faq.a: 2 days are usually enough to cover the top attractions of Hanle Monastery at a relaxed pace.

faq.q: How much does a hotel cost in Hanle Monastery?
faq.a: Stays in Hanle Monastery start from ₹900 per night, with options across 4 price categories.

faq.q: What are the top places to visit in Hanle Monastery?
faq.a: The top attractions are Hanle (village), Major Atmospheric Cerenkov Experiment Telescope, Anlay.
